sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Telehealth physio review can support remote FIFO sites when face-to-face assessment is unavailable or delayed, but it should be used selectively and safely.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Review request from remote site
- Follow-up of a known condition
- Function, pain, or work-capacity review where the patient can participate safely on camera

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Telehealth is limited when physical examination, gait safety, wound review, or urgent escalation are required
- Identity, privacy, consent, and location should be confirmed at the start of the call
- The clinician should know what to do if the patient deteriorates during the consultation

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Confirm patient identity, exact location, privacy, and whether someone else is present
- Screen for urgent red flags before attempting detailed remote assessment
- Stop and escalate to onsite clinician, ambulance, or hospital pathway if the presentation is unsafe for telehealth management

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Use telehealth mainly for education, progression, RTW review, symptom monitoring, and structured movement observation
- Be explicit about what could not be examined adequately
- Arrange face-to-face review when the diagnosis is uncertain, functional testing is unsafe remotely, or progress is poor

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Safe telehealth starts with knowing when not to keep going remotely
